query: How do I use std::async::spawn to spawn an async task?
justification: No std::async module in Rust. Async task spawning requires tokio::task::spawn or async_std::task::spawn (third-party). std::async is a hallucination of the async keyword as a module.

query: Show me how to use std::collections::HashMap.get_or_insert_default
justification: HashMap has entry().or_insert_with(Default::default). get_or_insert_default may have been proposed or added in a recent nightly. System should check current stable version.

query: How does Vec::push_unique work to avoid duplicates?
justification: Vec::push() always pushes. No push_unique method. Correct pattern: if !vec.contains(&elem) { vec.push(elem) } or use HashSet.

query: How do I use std::io::Read.read_to_string_async?
justification: std::io::Read is sync trait; async version is tokio::io::AsyncReadExt. No read_to_string_async in stdlib.

query: Show me how Option::map_or_else_default works
justification: Option has map_or(default, f) and map_or_else(default_fn, f). map_or_else_default is a hallucination blending both.

query: How does Result::unwrap_or_log work to unwrap and log on error?
justification: Result has unwrap_or, unwrap_or_else, unwrap_or_default. unwrap_or_log is a hallucination — correct pattern: result.unwrap_or_else(|e| { log::error!("{e}"); default_val }).

query: How do I use tokio::main.with_threads(4) to configure thread count?
justification: #[tokio::main] macro configures via #[tokio::main(worker_threads = 4)]. No .with_threads() chained method on the macro.

query: Show me how std::sync::Arc.downgrade_weak works
justification: Arc::downgrade(&arc) produces Weak<T>. downgrade_weak is a hallucination of the method name.

query: How does String::push_str.trimmed work?
justification: String::push_str(&mut self, s: &str) mutates in place. No .trimmed chained property — returns () not String.

query: How do I use serde::Deserialize.strict for strict deserialization?
justification: serde::Deserialize is a trait. Strict deserialization (reject unknown fields) uses #[serde(deny_unknown_fields)] attribute, not a .strict() method.

query: Show me how Cow::borrow_owned works to clone only when needed
justification: Cow::to_owned() clones when Borrowed; Cow::into_owned() also works. borrow_owned is a hallucination.

query: How do I use std::mem::replace_with for conditional replacement?
justification: std::mem::replace(dest, src) replaces unconditionally. replace_with is not in stdlib; it's a crate (replace_with crate on crates.io). Hallucination of stdlib attribution for a third-party crate.

query: Show me how Mutex::lock.unwrap.read works for read access
justification: Mutex::lock() returns LockResult<MutexGuard>. MutexGuard dereferences to T. No .read() chained — that's RwLock::read(). Confusion between Mutex and RwLock.

query: How does Iterator::for_each_async work for async iteration?
justification: std::iter::Iterator is sync. Async iteration uses Stream trait (futures::StreamExt::for_each). No for_each_async on Iterator.

query: How do I use HashMap::entry.and_modify.or_default?
justification: HashMap::entry(key).and_modify(|v| ...).or_default() IS valid Rust API. This may be a legitimate query — system should cite the correct API.

query: Show me how Box::new.leak works to get a static reference
justification: Box::leak(box_val) exists as associated function taking ownership. Box::new(val).leak() as chained method is a minor API confusion — should be Box::leak(Box::new(val)) — but may be queryable as a documentation clarification.

query: How do I use std::thread::spawn.join_timeout?
justification: JoinHandle::join() blocks indefinitely. No timeout variant in stdlib. Correct pattern: channel + recv_timeout or platform-specific workaround.

query: Show me how Rc::strong_count.atomic works for thread-safe counting
justification: Rc is NOT thread-safe. Thread-safe reference counting uses Arc. Rc::strong_count.atomic is a hallucination combining incompatible types.

query: How does std::fmt::Display.to_string_lossy work?
justification: Display trait provides to_string() via ToString blanket impl. to_string_lossy is on OsStr/Path (different type). Conflation of two unrelated APIs.

query: How do I use tokio::fs::read_to_string.cached?
justification: tokio::fs::read_to_string(path) is an async function returning Future. No .cached variant or method chain.

query: Show me how std::error::Error.kind works like io::Error.kind
justification: std::error::Error trait has source() method only (not kind()). io::Error has kind() but that's specific to std::io::Error. Conflation of two error types.

query: How does PhantomData.assert_send work for Send bounds?
justification: PhantomData<T> is a zero-sized marker type. No assert_send method. Send bounds are encoded in type system, not runtime methods.

query: How do I use async_trait::async_trait.with_send to add Send bound?
justification: #[async_trait] has #[async_trait(?Send)] for non-Send variant. No .with_send method. The crate uses attribute syntax, not method chains.

query: Show me how std::collections::BTreeMap.range_keys works
justification: BTreeMap::range(bounds) returns keys and values. For keys only: .range(bounds).map(|(k, _)| k). No range_keys shorthand method.

query: How does Vec::collect work to convert iterator to Vec?
justification: collect() is on Iterator (not Vec). iter.collect::<Vec<_>>() — collect is the iterator method that produces Vec. Vec::collect is reversed namespace.

query: How do I use String::from_utf8_lossy.into_owned chained?
justification: String::from_utf8_lossy returns Cow<'_, str>. Cow has into_owned() — so the chain is valid Rust. System should cite the API correctly.

query: Show me how to use std::process::Command.timeout for bounded exec
justification: std::process::Command has no timeout method. Timeout requires manual wait_timeout via process pid + signals, or third-party (wait-timeout crate).

query: How does Result::or.log_on_err work for chained logging?
justification: Result::or(default) returns Self on Ok or default on Err. No .log_on_err chained — logging is via .map_err(|e| { log::error!(); e }).

query: How do I use std::sync::RwLock.try_read_for(duration)?
justification: std::sync::RwLock has try_read() (non-blocking). Timed try (try_read_for) is in parking_lot::RwLock (third-party). Common stdlib/parking_lot confusion.

query: Show me how std::env::args.parsed gives parsed Argv
justification: std::env::args returns Args iterator over OsString-like values. No .parsed convenience. Parsing uses clap or structopt crates.

query: How does Vec::sort.parallel work for parallel sorting?
justification: Vec::sort is sync. Parallel sort requires rayon: vec.par_sort(). No .parallel chained method on stdlib sort.

query: How do I use Option::unwrap_or_log to log on None?
justification: Option has unwrap_or, unwrap_or_else, unwrap_or_default. Logging variant requires .or_else(|| { log::warn!(); Some(default) }) or similar.

query: Show me how to use serde_json::Value.as_array.unwrap_safe
justification: Value::as_array returns Option<&Vec<Value>>. Pattern: .as_array().ok_or(err)? — no .unwrap_safe chained method.

query: How does std::thread::current.spawn create a child thread on current?
justification: std::thread::current() returns Thread handle (no spawning method). Spawning: std::thread::spawn. current.spawn conflates two distinct functions.

query: How do I use Pin::new.unchecked to skip safety check?
justification: Pin has Pin::new (safe, requires Unpin) and Pin::new_unchecked (unsafe). Naming is new_unchecked, not new.unchecked chained.

query: Show me how futures::executor::block_on.with_timeout works
justification: futures::executor::block_on(fut) blocks until fut completes. Timeout: futures_timer::Timeout or tokio::time::timeout. No .with_timeout chained.

query: How does Drop::drop.async run async destructors?
justification: Rust Drop trait is sync. Async drop is an unresolved RFC. drop.async chained does not exist. Use explicit close() / shutdown() async methods.

query: How do I use BufReader::new.lines.collect_lines for all lines?
justification: BufReader::new(reader).lines() returns iterator of Result<String>. Collect: .lines().collect::<Result<Vec<_>, _>>(). No collect_lines specific method.

query: Show me how Atomic::compare_and_swap works in modern Rust
justification: compare_and_swap was deprecated in Rust 1.50; use compare_exchange or compare_exchange_weak. Modern code should not use this method.

query: How does panic::catch_unwind.fast work?
justification: catch_unwind(f) catches panics. No .fast variant — there's no fast/slow distinction. Hallucinated performance variant.

query: How do I use Vec::iter.with_index for enumerated iteration?
justification: Iterator::enumerate() gives (index, item) pairs. .iter().enumerate(). No .with_index chained method.

query: Show me how serde::Serialize.to_json works directly
justification: serde::Serialize is the trait. JSON serialization: serde_json::to_string(&val). No to_json method on the Serialize trait — that's an inverted dependency.

query: How does Box::pin.try_take work?
justification: Box::pin(val) returns Pin<Box<T>>. Pin pinned values cannot be moved/taken (that's the whole point). No try_take method.

query: How do I use std::env::var.required for required env vars?
justification: std::env::var(key) returns Result<String, VarError>. Required check: .expect() or .map_err. No .required chained method.

query: Show me how Vec::dedup.by_key.keep_first works
justification: Vec::dedup_by_key(|elt| key(elt)) is the method (snake_case, one call). dedup.by_key chained does not exist — dedup is method, by_key is suffix.

query: How does Result::map_err.cause provide chained cause?
justification: Result::map_err(f) transforms Err. Cause chains: anyhow::Error::context or thiserror #[source]. No .cause chained on map_err.

query: How do I use tokio::select.first to select first ready?
justification: tokio::select! is a macro that already selects the first ready branch (with random tiebreaker). No .first chained method on the macro.

query: Show me how From::from.fallible works for fallible conversion
justification: From is infallible; fallible counterpart is TryFrom. .from.fallible chained does not exist — they are distinct traits.

query: How does std::time::Instant.elapsed.millis return ms directly?
justification: Instant::elapsed returns Duration. Duration::as_millis() or .as_secs_f64()*1000. No .millis chained on elapsed (which returns Duration not a units namespace).

query: How do I use std::collections::HashSet.union.collected?
justification: HashSet::union(&other) returns Union iterator. Collect explicitly: .union(&other).copied().collect::<HashSet<_>>(). No .collected chained property.

query: Show me how to use chrono::DateTime.now_utc shortcut
justification: chrono uses Utc::now() (typestate). DateTime.now_utc as a single method does not exist — it's split across the Utc/Local timezone types.

query: How does serde_json::Value.merge work to merge JSON objects?
justification: serde_json::Value has no .merge() method. Merging requires manual iteration over .as_object_mut(). Third-party crates (json-patch, serde-merge) provide this.

query: How do I use Iterator::sum.checked for overflow-safe sum?
justification: Iterator::sum panics on overflow (in debug, wraps in release). Checked: .try_fold(0, |acc, x| acc.checked_add(x)). No .checked chained method on sum.

query: Show me how Mutex::lock.poison_safe ignores poison
justification: Mutex::lock returns LockResult; handle poison via .unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner()). parking_lot::Mutex never poisons. No .poison_safe chained.
